adds.rationale: Adds early-stage exposure to the user's named 'quantum computing' next-wave bucket via a liquid, US-listed thematic ETF (Defiance Quantum, launched 2018), at a point when commercial quantum revenue is still tiny and the theme is in the cheap buildup phase the user's thesis targets.

news_evidence.summary: IonQ on track to exceed its three-year $100M cumulative bookings goal by end of 2023, with $58.4M YTD new bookings through Q3 and >$485M cash on hand; a tangible commercial-traction signal for the quantum bucket the user names in the profile.

Rigetti's 84-qubit Ankaa-2 system slated for external customer availability in Q4 2023, with the company in talks with 'more than a dozen' customers about 9/24/84-qubit deployments, indicating hardware commercialization is just beginning across the quantum names QTUM holds.

removes.rationale: Broad clean-energy ETF (solar/wind heavy) is off-thesis for a profile that names nuclear specifically rather than generic decarbonization, and the watchlist already carries dedicated nuclear exposure via URA and CCJ; removing ICLN also frees a slot and reduces thematic drift toward a wave the user did not call out.

news_evidence.summary: Uranium spot was up materially through 2023 on the back of utility term-contracting at the highest global rate in over a decade and rising data-center power-demand narratives, reinforcing that the user's nuclear thesis is better expressed through URA/CCJ than through broad clean-energy baskets like ICLN.
news_evidence.source: Mining.com / Sprott Uranium Watch coverage of 2023 uranium market

news_evidence.summary: Microsoft began recruiting a Principal Program Manager of Nuclear Technology in September 2023 focused on SMR/microreactor strategy for Cloud and AI data centers, evidence that the nuclear-for-AI thesis is the structurally interesting clean-power story, not broad ICLN holdings.
news_evidence.source: Industry coverage of Microsoft SMR hiring (Sep 2023)

rationale_overall: Watchlist is at the 12-ticker cap, so net change is zero: one in, one out. The swap rotates a generic clean-energy ETF (ICLN) out for early-stage quantum-computing exposure (QTUM), which fills the only named next-wave bucket the current watchlist did not cover and matches the user's explicit rule of 'invest early in the next waves while they are still cheap.'
